microservice_supervisor.stdout:
#!/bin/bash

SC_ROOT="/opt/sc"
SCUSER="tns"
BINSERVICESDIR="$SC_ROOT/bin/services"
DAEMONSDIR="$SC_ROOT/daemons"
SERVICES=('sc-asset-svc')
SERVICES_PIDFILES=('sc-asset-svc.pid')
SVC_SUPERVISOR_PIDFILE="${DAEMONSDIR}/microservice-supervisor.pid"
SVC_SUPERVISOR_LOG="${SC_ROOT}/admin/logs/services/microservice-supervisor.log"

BASE_BACKOFF=30
MAX_BACKOFF=300

declare -A SERVICE_PORTS
SERVICE_PORTS=(
	["sc-asset-svc"]="8840"
)

declare -A FAILED_ATTEMPTS
declare -A LAST_ATTEMPT_TIME

for service in "${SERVICES[@]}"; do
	FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service]=0
	LAST_ATTEMPT_TIME[$service]=0
done

date_log()
{
	local message="$1"
	printf "[%s]: %s\n" "$(date +"%d-%b-%Y %H:%M:%S.%3N %Z")" "$message" >>"$SVC_SUPERVISOR_LOG"
}

check_port_availability()
{
	local port="$1"
	local in_use
	in_use=$(lsof -i :"${port}" -t 2>/dev/null)

	if [ -n "$in_use" ]; then
		local using_process
		using_process=$(ps -p "$in_use" -o comm= 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown process")
		date_log "Unexpected process using port ${port} - PID ${in_use} (${using_process}). Please stop the process before starting the service."
		return 1
	fi

	return 0
}

calculate_backoff()
{
	local attempts=$1
	local backoff=$((BASE_BACKOFF * (2 ** attempts)))

	# Cap at maximum backoff
	if [ "$backoff" -gt "$MAX_BACKOFF" ]; then
		backoff=$MAX_BACKOFF
	fi

	echo $backoff
}

cleanup()
{
	date_log "Supervisor shutting down."

	for pidfile in "${SERVICES_PIDFILES[@]}"; do
		full_pidfile="${DAEMONSDIR}/${pidfile}"
		[ -f "$full_pidfile" ] || continue
		pid=$(cat "$full_pidfile" 2>/dev/null)

		if [ -n "$pid" ] && kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
			date_log "Terminating child process with PID $pid from $full_pidfile"
			kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null
		else
			date_log "PID file $full_pidfile is empty or process is no longer running."
		fi

		rm -f "$full_pidfile"
	done

	[ -f "$SVC_SUPERVISOR_PIDFILE" ] && rm -f "$SVC_SUPERVISOR_PIDFILE"
	exit 0
}

supervise_microservices()
{
	date_log "Supervisor started."
	trap cleanup SIGINT SIGTERM EXIT

	while [ -f "$SVC_SUPERVISOR_PIDFILE" ]; do
		for ((i = 0; i < ${#SERVICES[@]}; i++)); do
			local service="${SERVICES[$i]}"
			local pidfile="${DAEMONSDIR}/${SERVICES_PIDFILES[$i]}"
			local port="${SERVICE_PORTS[$service]}"
			local needs_restart=0
			local current_time=$(date +%s)

			if [ -f "$pidfile" ]; then
				local pid
				pid=$(cat "$pidfile")
				if ! kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
					date_log "Micro-service $service (PID: $pid) died - attempting restart"
					needs_restart=1
					rm -f "$pidfile"
				else
					# Service is running, reset backoff
					FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service]=0
					LAST_ATTEMPT_TIME[$service]=0
				fi
			else
				date_log "Micro-service $service has no PID file - attempting restart"
				needs_restart=1
			fi

			if [ "$needs_restart" -eq 1 ]; then
				local backoff_seconds=0

				if [ "${FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service]}" -gt 0 ]; then
					backoff_seconds=$(calculate_backoff "${FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service]}")
					local elapsed_seconds=$((current_time - LAST_ATTEMPT_TIME[$service]))

					if [ "$elapsed_seconds" -lt "$backoff_seconds" ]; then
						date_log "Skipping restart of service: $service - in backoff period. Next attempt in $((backoff_seconds - elapsed_seconds)) seconds"
						continue
					fi
				fi

				date_log "Auto-restarting micro-service: $service (attempt ${FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service]})"
				LAST_ATTEMPT_TIME[$service]=$current_time

				if ! check_port_availability "$port"; then
					FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service]=$((FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service] + 1))
					date_log "Port check failed for service: $service. Backoff set to $(calculate_backoff "${FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service]}") seconds"
					continue
				fi

				if [ ! -x "$BINSERVICESDIR/$service" ]; then
					date_log "Binary $BINSERVICESDIR/$service not found or not executable."
					FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service]=$((FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service] + 1))
					date_log "Restart failed for service: $service. Backoff set to $(calculate_backoff "${FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service]}") seconds"
					continue
				fi

				"$BINSERVICESDIR/$service" >>"$SC_ROOT/admin/logs/services/${service}.log" 2>&1 &
				local new_pid=$!
				echo "$new_pid" >"$pidfile"
				sleep 3

				if kill -0 "$new_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
					date_log "Successfully restarted $service with PID: $new_pid"
					FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service]=0
				else
					date_log "Failed to restart $service"
					FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service]=$((FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service] + 1))
					date_log "Backoff set to $(calculate_backoff "${FAILED_ATTEMPTS[$service]}") seconds"
					rm -f "$pidfile"
				fi
			fi
		done

		sleep 30
	done

	date_log "Micro-service supervisor pidfile is missing - exiting."
	cleanup
}

# Write supervisor PID and start supervision
echo $$ >"$SVC_SUPERVISOR_PIDFILE"
supervise_microservices

health_summary.interpretation: Tenable.sc 6.8 uses PostgreSQL for modern finding/asset/plugin storage while this lab still has SQLite databases for application, organization, asset-list and legacy/local state. This is expected because the container was previously on a 6.6-era SQLite-backed release.
